_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 Texas State Representatives (Austin/Williamson/Hill Country). Currency: Hinojosa = 2026 Dem nominee for GOVERNOR (won primary, faces Abbott); Talarico = 2026 Dem nominee for U.S. SENATE (faces Paxton); both no longer seeking House. Harris Davila won 2026 GOP primary. Only TRUE/FALSE cells set; reset_unspecified nulls all other applicable cells (clears prior party-default inferences).

set.office: Texas State Representative, District 49 (sitting · 2026 Democratic nominee for Governor)

gina-hinojosa@TX.notes_set: Sitting HD-49 rep (Austin) since 2017; won 2026 Democratic gubernatorial primary 3/3/2026, faces Greg Abbott 11/3/2026 (not seeking House re-election). Documented progressive record: voted NO on SB8 heartbeat & HB1280 (2021); Texas Alliance for Life 89th scorecard 44%; endorsed by EMILY's List and Planned Parenthood Texas Votes; led House opposition to SB2 ESA/voucher; endorsed by Houston LGBTQ+ Political Caucus; defended drive-thru/24-hr voting via 2021 SB1 walkout; publicly opposes permitless carry ('convict carry'). No evidence found on euthanasia, Christian-liberty votes, public justice, federalism, border, or most gun cells — nulled.

0.note: Voted NO on SB8 (6-week heartbeat) and HB1280 (criminal abortion penalties), 2021; pro-choice record.

1.note: Does not advocate abolition; 44% pro-life score, voted against pro-life bills (SB31/SB33/HB7) in 89th session.

4.note: Endorsed/backed by EMILY's List (1/28/2026) and Planned Parenthood Texas Votes.

0.note: Endorsed by Houston LGBTQ+ Political Caucus; pro-LGBTQ record — does not affirm man/woman-only marriage.

4.note: Filed HB73 banning 'gay/trans panic' defense; LGBTQ caucus endorsement — supports LGBTQ in policy.

0.note: Voted NO on SB2 ESA/voucher (2025), led committee opposition; authored public-school funding act; calls vouchers a 'scam'.

2.note: Participated in 2021 Democratic walkout opposing SB1, which curtailed drive-thru/24-hr voting; favors expanded access.

3.note: Opposed SB1 limits on drive-thru/24-hr voting via 2021 walkout.

0.note: Publicly opposes permitless/constitutional carry: 'I don't believe there is such a thing as constitutional carry... I call it convict carry.'

set.office: Texas State Representative, District 50 (sitting · running for higher office 2026)

james-talarico@TX.notes_set: Sitting HD-50 rep (North Austin) since 2018; won 2026 Democratic U.S. Senate primary 3/3/2026, faces Ken Paxton 11/3/2026 (not seeking House re-election). Documented progressive record: pro-abortion-access (PP Texas Votes endorsed; TAL 42%); voted AGAINST SB14 youth gender-medicine ban (2023); leading voucher opponent (anti-voucher 'Friendly' rating, Parent PAC); opposed SB10 Ten Commandments mandate (framed in Christian terms — scored on the policy, not rhetoric); 0%/F from NRA & Texas State Rifle Assn, voted against HB1927 permitless carry, supports red-flag laws. Public-justice 'pro-police/pro-bail' claims are contested campaign spin (SOFT) — nulled. Election, federalism cells nulled (no HARD vote located).

0.note: Pro-abortion-access; pushed ballot measure for abortion rights and repeal of pre-Roe statutes; TAL 42%.

1.note: Does not support abolition; pro-choice voting record.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes (2024).

2.note: Voted AGAINST SB14 (2023 ban on youth gender medicine): 'this is not about science or medicine... not about keeping kids safe.'

0.note: Leading voucher opponent; anti-voucher 'Friendly' rating, Texas Parent PAC endorsed; backed Herrero anti-voucher amendment.

3.note: Opposed SB10 Ten Commandments in classrooms (called it 'unconstitutional'); against public-square religious display mandate.

1.note: 2019 social posts advising undocumented immigrants how to avoid ICE; opposes mass-deportation enforcement.

0.note: Voted to strike enacting clause of HB1927 permitless carry and against passage (2021); 0%/F from NRA & TSRA.

1.note: Supports red-flag laws; co-authored HB220/HB2744 gun-control bills (2023).

lulu-flores@TX.notes_set: Sitting HD-51 rep (E. Travis/Austin) since 2023; on 2026 Democratic primary ballot for re-election. Documented progressive record: pro-abortion-access ('autonomy of women to make decisions'); member of Texas House LGBTQ Caucus and endorsed by Texas Freedom Network; voted NO on SB2 ESA/voucher (prioritizes public-ed funding). Texas Alliance for Life 45% is a scoring artifact, not a pro-life signal. No HARD roll calls located on SB14/SB10/SB11; Christian-liberty, economic, public-justice, federalism, border, and 2A cells nulled (no evidence; not in office for HB1927).

0.note: Pro-choice: 'recognizing the autonomy of women to make decisions for themselves'; backs expanding physician abortion discretion.

1.note: Supports expanding abortion access; does not advocate abolition.

0.note: Member of Texas House LGBTQ Caucus; endorsed by Texas Freedom Network — does not affirm man/woman-only marriage.

1.note: Member of Texas House LGBTQ Caucus.

4.note: LGBTQ Caucus member; TFN endorsement — supports LGBTQ in policy.

0.note: Voted NO on SB2 ESA/voucher (89th session); prioritizes increasing public-education funding.

caroline-harris-davila@TX.notes_set: Sitting HD-52 rep (Williamson County/Round Rock) since 2023; won 2026 GOP primary, faces Democrat Chris Jimenez 11/3/2026. Documented social-conservative record (Texas Values 94%, Texas Alliance for Life 93%, Texas Right to Life pro-life leader): voted FOR SB33 (no taxpayer abortion funds), SB8 Women's Privacy, HB229 'What is a Woman Act', SB1257 Detransitioners, kept the homosexual-conduct statute (against HB1738 repeal), SB2 ESA voucher, SB12 Parents' Bill of Rights, SB13 library-content, SB10 Ten Commandments, SB11 prayer, SB965 Kennedy Law. CORRECTION: prior 'voted for constitutional-carry statute' claim removed — HB1927 passed 2021, before she took office (2023); 2A cells nulled. Public-justice/border/federalism prior all-TRUE were party-inferred (no cited votes) — reset to null.

0.note: Texas Alliance for Life 93%; Texas Right to Life-named pro-life leader; self-described 100% pro-life.

4.note: Texas Right to Life-endorsed Republican; no abortion-industry PAC funding found.

0.note: Voted to keep the homosexual-conduct statute (against HB1738 repeal); Texas Values 'Faith & Family Champion' 94%.

2.note: Voted FOR SB8 Women's Privacy Act, HB229 'What is a Woman Act', SB1257 Detransitioners Bill of Rights (2025).

4.note: Voted to keep homosexual-conduct statute; Texas Values 94% champion — opposes LGBTQ promotion in policy.

0.note: Voted YES on SB2 ESA/voucher (House 85-63, 4/17/2025); not among the two GOP no-votes.

1.note: Voted FOR SB12 Parents' Bill of Rights (2025).

2.note: Voted FOR SB13 (remove harmful content from school libraries) (2025).

0.note: Voted FOR SB965 'Coach Joe Kennedy Law' protecting religious expression.

3.note: Voted FOR SB10 Ten Commandments in classrooms and SB11 prayer in schools (2025).

wes-virdell@TX.notes_set: Sitting freshman HD-53 rep (Hill Country), took office 1/14/2025; former Gun Owners of America Texas state director. Documented record: Texas Right to Life 96% & Texas Alliance for Life 96%; authored HB3399 banning gender-transition care (all ages); voted YEA on SB2 ESA voucher (House journal Record 300, 4/17/2025) and SB10 Ten Commandments (Record 3488, 5/24/2025); GOA 'A' rating and named GOA Texas '89th Legislative Champion' on 2A. CORRECTION: a TCTA profile miscoded his SB2 vote as 'against'; the official House journal shows YEA — used the journal. His libertarian lean appears in property-rights bills (HB1379 limiting warrantless officer entry), not the culture-war flagships. Public-justice, election, federalism, border cells nulled (no cited votes).

0.note: Texas Right to Life 96% and Texas Alliance for Life 96% (89th session).

4.note: 96% pro-life Republican; no abortion-industry PAC funding found.

2.note: Authored HB3399 banning gender-transition care for all ages; rejects transgender medical interventions.

0.note: Voted YEA on SB2 ESA/voucher final passage (House journal Record 300, 4/17/2025); only Phelan & VanDeaver voted no among Rs.

3.note: Voted YEA on SB10 Ten Commandments in classrooms (House journal Record 3488, 5/24/2025).

0.note: Former GOA Texas state director; GOA 'A' rating and '89th Legislative Champion' — constitutional carry is his signature issue.

1.note: GOA 'Legislative Champion' — opposes red-flag laws, magazine limits, AWB, and registries (GOA core positions).
